title: 3 Quiet Yeses in Unanswered Prayer
hook: Unanswered? Or answered differently? Spot these 3 quiet yeses.
script: Unanswered? Or answered differently? Spot these 3 quiet yeses. First, the yes of strength: like Paul in 2 Corinthians 12:9, God gives grace to endure while shaping you. Second, the yes of redirection: Proverbs 16:9 says God establishes our steps, even through closed doors. Third, the yes of timing and formation: Habakkuk 2:3 and James 1 say wait; maturity needs time. Test it today: Am I being sustained? Are detours guiding me to wiser ground? Is patience growing? Start a simple prayer log. Date your request. Note strength, reroutes, and slow wins. You may discover God has been saying yes all along.

title: When God's Silence Is a Yes
hook: If God feels silent right now, watch this.
script: If God feels silent right now, watch this. Silence can still be a yes. Sometimes God says yes by strengthening you in the struggle, just like 2 Corinthians 12:9. Sometimes He says yes by closing one door to lead you to a better one, Proverbs 16:9. And sometimes His yes is not yet, because He is forming you, Habakkuk 2:3 and James 1. Pray this today: Lord, show me your yes. Take one small step that matches Scripture and wisdom. Tonight, title a page with your request, then track strength you feel, doors that shift, and patience that grows. Give it seven days. Watch what changes.

title: Hidden Yes While You Wait
hook: If you're tired of waiting on God, listen to this.
script: If you're tired of waiting on God, listen to this. You may already be living in a quiet yes. Notice the grace to keep going when you planned to quit; that's 2 Corinthians 12:9 in motion. Notice the unexpected route that fits you better; Proverbs 16:9 says God directs steps. Notice the slow build that is producing steadier character; Habakkuk 2:3 and James 1 call that maturity. Test your situation with two questions: Does this align with Scripture? Is it growing love, patience, and humility in me? If so, it might be God's yes. Start a prayer log and date today. In a month, compare. You'll see the thread.
